ASOS STATION KGEY GREYBULL AIRPORT.
REPORT OF 82 MPH WIND GUST WITH MANY TREES DOWN AND POWER KNOCKED OUT. EAST ENTRANCE OF YELLOWSTONE CLOSED UNTIL 7PM MDT DUE TO AVALANCHE.
ASOS STATION KRKS ROCK SPRINGS AIRPORT.
ASOS STATION KBPI BIG PINEY AIRPORT.
ONE INCH DIAMETER HAIL REPORTED 3 MILES NORTH OF BASIN ALONG WYOMING HIGHWAY 789.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL. THERE ARE ALSO NUMEROUS REPORTS ON NICKEL SIZE HAIL AROUND MIDWEST AND EDGERTON.
REPORT OF LARGE HAIL DENTING VEHICLE HOOD WHILE DRIVING OVER TETON PASS. ESTIMATING AT LEAST 1 INCH DIAMETER HAIL BASED ON REPORT OF DAMAGE.

LOCAL EMERGENCY MANAGER FORWARDED A SOCIAL MEDIA POST SHOWING A LARGE PINE TREE SNAPPED NEAR THE BASE AND LYING ACROSS A ROAD IN THE TOWN OF JACKSON, WY. DIAMETER OF TRUCK WAS ABOUT 18 INCHES, ESTIMATED FROM PICTURE.

APPROXIMATE 3 MILE PATH OF OLD GROWTH FIR TREES IN THE BRIDGER-TETON NATIONAL FOREST WERE SNAPPED, BLOWN OVER, OR UPROOTED DUE TO VERY STRONG WINDS FROM A SUPERCELL THUNDERSTORM. NWS EMPLOYEES ESTIMATE WIND SPEEDS FROM 80 TO 120 MPH FROM A STORM SURVEY. GROS VENTRE RIVER ROAD WAS BLOCKED DUE TO DOWNED TREES, AND THE CRYSTAL CREEK CAMPGROUND WAS SEVERELY IMPACTED, LIKELY CLOSED FOR THE REST OF THE SUMMER. ADDITIONALLY, PORTIONS OF THE GRIZZLY LAKE TRAIL SW OF THE CAMPGROUND WERE BLOCKED.
TREE DAMAGE AND A POWER LINE DOWN FROM STRONG WIND.
ASOS STATION KGEY GREYBULL AIRPORT.
REPORT FROM MPING: 1-INCH TREE LIMBS BROKEN; SHINGLES BLOWN OFF.
